extra.reasoning: The acuity output depends only on sweating and dyspnea. The input variable `fever` does not appear in any rule condition or in the default, so it has no effect on acuity. Both rules (raise_sweating, raise_dyspnea, each priority 3) set acuity := emergent when their condition holds; otherwise the default sets acuity := routine. Enumerating all fixed (sweating, dyspnea) pairs: (F,F)->routine, (F,T)->emergent, (T,F)->emergent, (T,T)->emergent. Flipping fever from false to true leaves the output identical in every case (sweating and dyspnea unchanged), so acuity is never lowered. The monotonicity property (acuity_after >= acuity_before) is satisfied — equality holds throughout. The property holds over the entire input space.
